beats.text: Life on Earth started nearly 4 billion years ago — but complex cells? Only 1.5 to 2 billion years back. A timeline appears, marking both moments.
Why the gap? Bacteria already owned the planet. A crowd of bacterial cells fills the frame, packed wall to wall. No room for newcomers.
Now zoom out — an MIT student stands in a forest. Labels cascade around her: ecosystem, organism, organ, tissue, cell.
Zoom into one photoreceptor. A mitochondrion pulses inside, stamping out ATP molecules one by one.
Life is nested containers, all the way down. Each layer locks into the next.
And the molecular rules inside that one cell? Nearly identical across every living thing. Master the cell — you unlock all of life.
